title: Wait & Hold Experience
lede: Silence and endless ringing make callers hang up — a polished wait experience keeps them on the line. In a few minutes you'll set the music and spoken messages your callers hear while the group rings, while they wait in a queue, and when an agent puts them on hold.

steps.title: Open the Wait & Hold experience tab
steps.content:
Everything on this page is configured per group, so each team can have its own sound.

  1. In the left sidebar, under Call Center, click Contact Center.
  2. Open the Groups tab at the top of the page.
  3. In the Groups list on the left, click the group you want to configure.
  4. In the group panel, open the Wait & Hold experience tab (if you don't see it, click the … overflow at the end of the tab bar).

You'll land on the Caller wait & hold experience card with four controls: Play wait audio during the initial group ring, Wait music, Periodic messages, and Hold music.

images.alt: Wait & Hold experience tab inside a Contact Center group
images.caption: The Wait & Hold experience tab: ring audio mode, wait music, periodic messages, and hold music.

images.alt: Wait & Hold experience tab inside a Contact Center group
images.caption: The Wait & Hold experience tab: ring audio mode, wait music, periodic messages, and hold music.

steps.title: Know the three moments a caller can hear this audio
steps.content:
Before you flip any switches, it helps to know exactly when each sound plays:

  • During the initial ring — while iMash rings your group's members. By default the caller hears normal phone ringing; you can replace it with your wait audio (next step).
  • While queued — when the caller is held in line for the next free agent (for example, a queue transfer, or when every agent is busy). Your Wait music and Periodic messages play here too.
  • On hold — when an agent presses Hold during a live call, the caller hears your Hold music.
💡 The queue has extra, queue-only options — max wait time, timeout action, and separate queue announcements with a live {callers_queue_position} token. Those live on the Queue Settings tab, covered in its own guide.

steps.title: Choose what plays during the initial group ring
steps.content:
The Play wait audio during the initial group ring dropdown decides what the caller hears while your team's phones are ringing:

  • Off — normal phone ringing (default) — the caller hears standard ringback until someone answers.
  • Only when a member is online — plays your wait audio instead of ringing, but only when at least one group member is online to take the call.
  • Always (even if nobody is online) — plays your wait audio on every call, even with no one online.
⚠️ Turning this on answers the call immediately so your music and messages can play instead of ringing — which means the call connects (and starts billing) from that moment. Leave it Off if you prefer the call to connect only when a human answers.

images.alt: Ring audio mode options: Off, Only when a member is online, Always
images.caption: The three ring-audio modes: normal ringing, only when a member is online, or always.

images.alt: Ring audio mode options: Off, Only when a member is online, Always
images.caption: The three ring-audio modes: normal ringing, only when a member is online, or always.

steps.title: Add wait music
steps.content:
Wait music is the soundtrack while the group is being rung or the caller is queued. To set it up:

  1. Switch on the Wait music toggle — an upload area appears.
  2. Click Upload wait music and pick an audio file (MP3 works great), or paste a link in the …or paste an audio URL field if your track is already hosted online.
💡 The upload limit is 10 MB — plenty, since a 128 kbps MP3 is only about 1 MB per minute. Use a short, pleasant loop (30–90 seconds); the track repeats automatically for as long as the caller waits.

images.alt: Wait music toggled on with Upload wait music button and audio URL field
images.caption: Switch on Wait music, then upload a track or paste an audio URL.

images.alt: Wait music toggled on with Upload wait music button and audio URL field
images.caption: Switch on Wait music, then upload a track or paste an audio URL.

steps.title: Add spoken periodic messages
steps.content:
Music alone is fine — music plus a friendly voice is better. Periodic messages are short lines spoken over the wait experience on a loop: they play in order, each after its own interval, then the cycle repeats.

  1. Switch on the Periodic messages toggle.
  2. (Optional) Tick Play the first message once before the wait music starts to turn message 1 into a one-time greeting — it gets a blue Before music tag and plays immediately, before any music.
  3. Type each message in its text box (up to 300 characters). Personalization tokens like {first_name} are filled in automatically for the caller, e.g. “Thanks for waiting, {first_name} — you're next!”
  4. In the small field next to each message, set the seconds to wait before it plays — a single number like 30, or a range like 15-45 for a random wait each time (feels more natural than clockwork announcements).
  5. Click Add message to add more lines, or the red trash icon to remove one (the last remaining message can't be deleted).

images.alt: Periodic messages editor with intro-before-music checkbox, two message rows and intervals
images.caption: The periodic messages editor: an optional before-music greeting, then looping messages with per-message intervals.

images.alt: Periodic messages editor with intro-before-music checkbox, two message rows and intervals
images.caption: The periodic messages editor: an optional before-music greeting, then looping messages with per-message intervals.

steps.title: Set hold music
steps.content:
Hold music plays when an agent presses Hold during a live call — the caller hears your track instead of dead air.

  1. Switch on the Hold music toggle.
  2. Click Upload hold music and pick an audio file, or paste a link in the …or paste an audio URL field.
💡 You can reuse the same track as your wait music for a consistent brand sound, or pick something different — they're independent settings. This track is also what queued callers hear when the queue's Play hold music while queued option is on (see Queue Settings).

images.alt: Hold music toggled on with Upload hold music button and audio URL field
images.caption: Switch on Hold music and upload the track callers hear when an agent presses Hold.

images.alt: Hold music toggled on with Upload hold music button and audio URL field
images.caption: Switch on Hold music and upload the track callers hear when an agent presses Hold.

steps.title: Save and hear it for yourself
steps.content:
1. Click Save in the bottom-right corner — one Save commits every tab of the group's settings.
  2. Test it like a customer: call one of the group's inbound numbers (set on the Inbound SIP Routing tab) and let it ring without answering. You should hear your wait music and messages exactly as configured.
  3. To test hold music, answer a group call from the web dialer and press Hold — the caller side switches to your hold track.

That's it — from the first ring to the moment an agent picks up, your callers now hear a professional, on-brand experience instead of silence. 🎶

nextSteps.title: What's Next?
nextSteps.description: You've perfected what callers hear — now decide how long they wait and what happens when the wait runs long.

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials

metaDescription: Set the wait music, spoken periodic messages, and hold music callers hear while ringing a Contact Center group, waiting in queue, or on hold in iMash.
